abstract: This community service initiative seeks to foster independence and entrepreneurial character among students at Pondok Pesantren Nurul Amanah Bangkalan by leveraging the Asset-Based Community Development (ABCD) framework. Engaging 10th and 11th-grade students, the program integrated vocational training within the pesantren’s existing business infrastructure, including ventures such as Bakso Gibras, Nasi Ayam Chili Padi, bottled water production, mushroom cultivation, and catfish farming. The pedagogical approach combined theoretical instruction on *mu'amalah*—grounding entrepreneurship in Sharia principles—with immersive, hands-on experience in daily business operations. Findings indicate that students not only actively contributed to these business units but also developed a sophisticated ability to navigate operational challenges and identify market opportunities. By utilizing the ABCD approach, the program successfully equipped students with practical business competencies while reinforcing the social and religious values central to the pesantren’s mission. Ultimately, this initiative empowered the student body and transformed the institution's commercial units into sustainable platforms for experiential learning and economic growth.
